description: The Level-1 analyst's human disposition of a screening hit that the engine gate routed for review. Open this only after keystone-screening-gate-v1 returned FAILED (score above the clear threshold); auto-cleared screens are terminal and never reach here. A screening analyst (the performer) assembles the comparison; a Level-1 analyst principal renders the binding trueMatch/falsePositive verdict. AGLedger holds the verdict and notarizes it with the analyst's attribution against the list version; the analyst decides whether the hit is real. False positives are expected, and each is dispositioned with a rationale so the record shows which analyst dismissed which alert, against which list version, on what basis. Separation of duties is a deployment control: the verdict is rendered by a distinct principal identity (the L1-analyst agent key), separate from the orchestrator/performer that ran the screen. For production attribution of the named analyst, bind the verdict via AGLedger-On-Behalf-Of (org IdP).

screenRef.description: recordId of the keystone-sanctions-screen-v1 being dispositioned.

screeningGateRef.description: recordId of the FAILED keystone-screening-gate-v1 that routed this hit to a human.

listVersion.description: The dataset/list snapshot the disposition is rendered against (must match the screen's datasetVersion).

disposition.description: trueMatch = the subject is the designated/listed party; falsePositive = the screen hit a different person/entity.

rationale.description: Why this is a true match or false positive (e.g. DOB/nationality discriminators).

matchedDatasets.description: Which list(s) drove the disposition. Conditional-required (non-empty) on a trueMatch: a confirmed sanctions match records which list it matched (the load-bearing audit fact and the input the OFAC-block report needs).
